yaml.title: Module 6: Graduate Academic Affairs
yaml.subtitle: Formatting, ProQuest submission, and graduation requirements

markdown.markdown:
::: {.learning-objectives} After completing this module, you will be able to: - Understand UCR's thesis and dissertation formatting requirements - Navigate the ProQuest electronic submission process - Identify key milestones and deadlines in the graduation timeline - Know which office to contact for graduation-related questions ::: --- ## Overview If you are completing your thesis or dissertation at UC Riverside, you will first prepare your manuscript using the **formatting guidelines provided by the Graduate Council**. Before submitting, carefully review the requirements or attend a formatting workshop to ensure your document meets all university standards. Once your committee has approved your final manuscript, submit it electronically via **ProQuest** for a final format review. After approval and all required forms are completed, your thesis or dissertation will be officially filed and you will be cleared for degree conferral. > ✅ Complete the assignment at the end of this module to earn credit toward your certificate! --- ## Formatting Requirements UCR Graduate Council sets specific formatting standards for all theses and dissertations. Key areas include: - **Margins:** Specific requirements for top, bottom, left, and right margins - **Font and size:** Approved fonts and minimum size requirements - **Spacing:** Line spacing requirements for body text, block quotes, and reference lists - **Page numbering:** Specific rules for preliminary pages vs. body pages - **Title page:** Required elements and exact formatting - **Table of contents:** Required formatting and depth ::: {.callout-warning} ### Attend a Format Workshop Before You Submit Formatting errors are one of the most common causes of delayed degree conferral. Graduate Division offers regular formatting workshops and detailed guides. **Do not wait until the last minute.** ::: --- ## The Submission Process ### Step-by-Step 1. **Draft and revise** your thesis/dissertation with your advisor and committee 2. **Attend a format workshop** or review formatting guidelines carefully 3. **Committee approval** — get required signatures on all forms 4. **ProQuest submission** — upload your final PDF for format review 5. **Format review** — Graduate Division reviews and may request revisions 6. **Final approval** — once format-approved and forms completed, you are cleared for degree conferral 7. **eScholarship deposit** — your work is automatically deposited (unless you opt for an embargo) ### Embargo Options You may request an **embargo** (delay in public access) if: - A publisher has requested exclusivity for publication - Your research contains sensitive data - You are filing patents related to your work Contact Graduate Division to discuss embargo options. --- ## ProQuest and Your Rights Submitting to ProQuest is a **graduation requirement**, but it is *not* the same as assigning all publishing rights. ProQuest's default agreement: - Makes your work available in their dissertation database - Allows you to **retain copyright** - Does not prevent you from publishing with a commercial publisher Always read the ProQuest publishing agreement before submitting. For questions, contact Jing Han ([jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu)). --- ## Key Contacts | Need | Contact | |---|---| | Formatting, forms, graduation timeline | [UCR Graduate Division](https://graduate.ucr.edu/) | | ProQuest questions, copyright in your thesis | Jing Han · [jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu) | | eScholarship deposit | Jing Han · [jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu) | --- ## Resources ::: {.resource-list} - [**Slides: Dissertation and Thesis Formatting and Submission Workshop**](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/16hibz_ztHns8QJ7vXH56b8SKDpIGXTKcgHePdPebA7U/edit?usp=sharing) [Slides]{.resource-type .slides} - [**Recording: Format Workshop for Your Thesis and Dissertation**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OP0ts5BLoXM) [Video]{.resource-type .video} - [**UCR Graduate Division Filing Resources**](https://graduate.ucr.edu/filing-resources#formatting-and-submission-link) — formatting guidelines, workshops, forms, and timelines [Guide]{.resource-type} ::: --- ## Assignment ::: {.callout-important} ### 📋 Assignment: Graduate Academic Affairs Knowledge Check Complete the knowledge check quiz in the [Knowledge Checks](../knowledge-checks.qmd) page. The quiz is also available on Canvas for credit. *This assignment is required for the certificate.* ::: --- ## Get Help - 📧 Formatting, forms, graduation timeline: [UCR Graduate Division](https://graduate.ucr.edu/) - 📧 ProQuest questions, copyright: Jing Han · [jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu) - 📧 eScholarship deposit: Jing Han · [jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu) --- ```{=html}
Final Step · Module 7
🎓 Capstone: Research Plan
→
```
